title: The Bells by Edgar Allan Poe — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Hear the sledges with the bells--
Silver bells!
What a world of merriment their melody foretells!
How they tinkle, tinkle, tinkle,
In their icy air of night!
While the stars, that oversprinkle
All the heavens, seem to twinkle
With a crystalline delight;
meaning: "The Bells" by Edgar Allan Poe is a sad and love and deep and nature poem consisting of 116 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Hear the sledges with the bells-- Silver bells!...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edgar Allan Poe's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
